tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Louisiana Man Becomes First in Gulf South Functionally Cured of Sickle Cell Disease
stories.summary: Daniel Cressy, who spent his first 23 years in and out of hospitals up to 12 times yearly, rang the bell at Manning Family Children's Hospital as Louisiana's first CRISPR gene therapy cure for sickle cell disease.

stories.headline: Best Friend Donates Her Kidney to Save a Five-Year-Old Girl's Life
stories.summary: Jamie Rogers, her mother's best friend, decided on her own to get tested and became a kidney match for 5-year-old Bree Bridges, who had spent a year and a half on nightly dialysis.

stories.headline: Maharashtra Police Officer Carries Elderly Woman Through Floodwaters on His Shoulders
stories.summary: The excerpt provided contains no specific rescue details—only boilerplate cookie policy language—so the summary remains unchanged.

stories.headline: ALS Patient Holds a Fork for the First Time in Four Years Thanks to Smart Glove
stories.summary: An ALS patient held a fork for the first time in four years using a smart glove with 97% accuracy, reading faint forearm signals to restore his grasp.

stories.headline: Young Catcher Honors Sister by Playing His MLB Debut the Day She Died
stories.summary: Dodgers catcher Eliezer Alfonzo Jr., 26, made his MLB debut hours after learning his 16-year-old sister Elaina died in a Venezuelan earthquake, stepping into the lineup with the crowd's heartfelt ovation.

stories.headline: Kindness Influencer Hands a Father and Sons the World Cup Tickets of Their Dreams
stories.summary: Kindness influencer Zachery Dereniowski gave Cormac Carville and his 14-year-old son Joshua World Cup tickets after Joshua spotted him in the Toronto crowd, turning an impossible £2,000 dream into reality.

stories.headline: 18-Year-Old Pitcher Throws 90 mph in Debut Just Six Months After Severe Car Crash
stories.summary: Frank Cairone defied recovery odds to return to the mound, proving that a shattered body can heal into something stronger.

stories.headline: Pub Landlord Paddles into Harbor to Free Injured Swan from Fishing Wire
stories.summary: Adam Szczypior, landlord of The Royal Oak, paddled his kayak into Langstone Harbour to free a male swan tangled in fishing wire with a large lure, reuniting him with his mate and four cygnets.

stories.headline: Nobel Laureate's Company Is Pulling Drinking Water from Desert Air Using Only Sunlight
stories.summary: Omar Yaghi's Atoco harnesses metal-organic frameworks to harvest clean water from thin air, promising water independence for the world's driest places.

stories.headline: Sea Turtle 'Soggy Bottom' Swims Back to Ocean After Months of Care
stories.summary: Soggy Bottom, a juvenile green sea turtle rescued in March after fishing line wrapped around her neck and flipper, was released back into Atlantic waters at Cocoa Beach after months of rehabilitation.

stories.headline: Cape Verde's Underdog Goalkeeper Earns Messi's Praise and the World's Heart
stories.summary: 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha of Cape Verde earned Lionel Messi's praise after his epic saves nearly toppled Argentina, with Messi telling him, 'Your people should be proud of you.'

stories.headline: Water Voles Return to North Devon After 25 Years of Absence
stories.summary: The National Trust released 110 water voles into North Devon wetlands after 25 years of absence, restoring habitats across the Combesgate catchment by planting 26,000 trees and sowing 15 hectares of wildflower seed.

stories.headline: Young Ospreys Released in Spain Return Triumphantly from Migration
stories.summary: The excerpt provided contains only boilerplate website introduction text with no specific osprey migration details—so the summary remains unchanged.
